caption: The powerpoint projected behind me on the smartboard was titled 'Advanced Thermodynamics,' but I wasn't paying attention to the equations. My focus was entirely on the heat radiating from the three students huddled around my desk, watching me demonstrate a principle using a heated beaker. My black slip dress, barely covering the curves beneath, felt impossibly cool against my skin, a stark contrast to the burning desire in the air. The professor next door could barely contain his gaze, and the one student in the front row, a shy freshman, was staring at the curve of my waist with an intensity that made my pulse quicken. As I bent down to adjust the temperature settings on the lab equipment, the fabric of my dress strained against my hips, the silhouette of my curves barely contained by the tailored cut. A low hum of tension filled the room, silent but palpable. I smiled, turning to face them, the dress hugging every line of my body as I leaned back in my chair, the fabric sliding slightly to reveal a hint of skin before settling back into place. 'The heat of the experiment,' I whispered, my voice barely audible, 'is just like the heat of the moment. You feel it, don't you?' The room erupted in a soft, collective hum. I laughed, the sound echoing through the glass walls as I stood, the dress rising slightly with my movement, before grabbing my coat. 'Class dismissed,' I purred, the image of me striding out of the room, hips swaying, the black silk clinging to every curve, burned into their minds forever.
